name: monday.com MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official monday.com MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with monday.com Work OS — creating and updating items, querying boards, managing columns, triggering automations, and integrating project data into agent workflows.
use_cases: Creating and updating project items from task management agents
Querying board data and status updates from project tracking agents
Automating item creation from incoming requests via pipeline agents
Reporting project health and progress from analytics agents
Moving items between groups/boards from workflow automation agents
Triggering monday.com automations from external agent events
not_for: Teams using Asana, Jira, ClickUp, or other project tools
CRM-primary use cases (use HubSpot or Salesforce)
best_when: An agent needs to manage work items, projects, or resources in a monday.com-powered organization.
avoid_when: You're using Jira, Asana, ClickUp, or another project management tool.

auth.notes: Personal API tokens or OAuth2 apps. OAuth2 scopes: boards:read, boards:write, users:read, etc. API v2 uses GraphQL exclusively.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 2 seats, 3 boards, basic features
pricing.notes: Per-seat pricing. Free plan available but limited. API access included in all paid plans. MCP server is open source.

known_agent_gotchas: monday.com uses GraphQL exclusively — no REST API, agents must know GraphQL
Board ID and item ID required for most mutations
Column types have different value formats (status, date, person, etc.)
Mutations return limited data — agents must re-query to confirm changes
Workspaces vs boards hierarchy — agents must discover board IDs first
Column values use JSON format specific to column type
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with granular scopes. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise SSO available.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with scopes. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise SSO.
